messages.content:
Which of the following sentence, related to the Economic Order Quantity (EOQ), is false?

Demand should be known, constant, and independent.

The total inventory cost should be minimum

The annual carrying cost and the annual ordering cost should be equal.

The safety stock should be estimated to reduce the risk of running out of stock during lead time.

The annual carrying cost and the annual ordering cost should be equal. (This statement is false. The total cost will be minimized when the carrying cost and the ordering cost are balanced at a specific quantity known as the economic order quantity or EOQ. It does not imply that they should be equal.)

The inventory investment metric that respond to following question: “How many times in a year the inventory was purchased and then sold?” is named:

The inventory turnover.

The inventory weeks of supply.

The inventory days of supply.

The day’s sales in inventory.
The inventory turnover.

You want an easy-to-use sales forecasting method that can account for seasonality. Which method would be best under these circumstances?

Moving Averages Method.

Delphi Method.

Decomposition Method.

Trend-adjusted Exponential Smoothing Method.

The Seasonal Index Method would be the best method in these circumstances, as it can account for seasonality by using seasonal indices. These indices adjust the forecast based on the pattern observed in historical data.

ABC analysis in materials management is a method of classifying the inventories based on

The value of annual usage of the items.

The Economic Order Quantity.

The Production Order Quantity.

The Quantity of material used.
The value of annual usage of the items.

In transportation problem with N supply points and M demand points, how many number of constraints are required in its Linear Programming formulation?

N – M

N + M – 1

N x M

None of the above responses.

In order for the time-series regression method to be used, you need?

Many periods of data to plot.

Seasonal Indices.

Customer survey results.

Good Statistical Software
Many periods of data to plot.

Which of the following is not a capacity option of production aggregate planning?

varying production rates through overtime or idle time.

changing inventory levels.

back ordering during high-demand periods.

subcontracting.
Backordering during high-demand periods is not a capacity option of production aggregate planning.

The ‘drum, buffer, rope’, concept is used in

The inventory theory and automated engineering concepts.

The theory of behavior and financial theory.

The disconfirmation theory.

The theory of constraints and optimized production technology.
The 'drum, buffer, rope' concept is used in the theory of constraints and optimized production technology.

Kanban is a system of

value engineering control.

quality control.

inventory control.

forecasting control.
Kanban is a system of inventory control, specifically a pull-based scheduling system used in Lean manufacturing and Just-in-Time (JIT) manufacturing.

In production planning, disaggregating the aggregate plan relates to:

yield management.

planning a chase approach.

master scheduling.

short-range planning.

In production planning, disaggregating the aggregate plan relates to master scheduling. It involves breaking down the aggregate plan into specific production orders for a particular period or week in the short term.

Closed Loop in materials requirements planning (MRP) means:

Unused materials are returned to stores and recorded back into the system.

Actual inventory is counted regularly and adjustment made to the inventory records.

Actual sales are netted off the forecasts in the Master production schedule.

Capacity and resource planning is included in the MRP logic.

Closed Loop in materials requirements planning (MRP) means actual inventory is counted regularly and adjustments are made to the inventory records based on the actual usage and transactions. The updated inventory data is then used to adjust the MRP calculations and ensure accuracy in the planning process.

Who of the following is mainly responsible for determining HOW the product is to be produced?

Product designer

Business owner

Process designer

Schedule designer
Process designer is mainly responsible for determining HOW the product is to be produced.

Which of the following is NOT part of the facilities design component of the facilities planning?

Handling system design

Facility systems design

Facilities location

Layout design
Facilities location is not part of the facilities design component of the facilities planning. It is classified under location planning.

In office facility planning, which of the following is an advantage of Open Offices?

Increase Privacy

Improve controlling noise

Improve supervision

All other choices

None of the choices are correct. The open office concept does not increase privacy, improve control over noise, or improve supervision. Instead, some of the claimed benefits of open offices include increased collaboration, a sense of community and teamwork, flexibility, and cost savings in terms of space and resources. However, evidence suggests that open offices can have negative effects on productivity, worker satisfaction, and wellness due to increased distractions and lack of privacy.

Which of the following is a factor that can affect the location selection?

Proximity to markets

Proximity to raw materials and suppliers

Labor costs

All other choices

All other choices can affect location selection, including but not limited to:

- Proximity to transportation and distribution channels
- Availability and cost of utilities and infrastructure
- Tax rates and incentives offered by the government or local community
- Environmental factors and zoning regulations
- Political stability and legal system
- Competitors and industry clusters in the area
- Cultural and social factors, including language and customs

Which of the following is a characteristic of large unit load?

Decreases work-in-process inventory

Support JIT production

Require bigger and heavier equipment

All other choices
Require bigger and heavier equipment is a characteristic of large unit load.

Why would a bill of materials be helpful in designing a facility?

Lists all the parts required to produce a product

Help in determining the storage requirements at each station

Help in determining the size of the receiving area

All other choices

A bill of materials would be helpful in designing a facility because it lists all the parts required to produce a product. This information can help in determining the layout of workstations, the storage requirements for materials and supplies, and the appropriate equipment and tools needed in the production process.

“a document that is created by simulationist to describe exactly what will be delivered, when, how, and by whom” is the definition of …

Stakeholder

Functional specification

Word document

Object
Functional specification

“Set of related components working together toward some purpose, usually over time” is the definition of …

Process

Event

System

Object
System
